sentence: Well, the family with the screaming baby moved.
So, how's Danny -- you two trying to work things out?
There's nothing to work out. He left.
I guess you could say I'm one of those people that's just better off alone.
So, how was the firing range?
How did it feel to hold a gun again?
All these steps count, Ana. You've come a long way since we met 4 months ago.
Did she say -- you know, when I can come back to work?
She left the final determination up to me.
Welcome back to the force, Officer Cortez.
Nobody move! Nobody! Does he have anything else on him?
He just had a gun to your neck -- check to see if he had anything else on him?
Are you crazy? Don't point that at me?
Get back! Get back, both of you!
We need to tie him up.
You, you do it. Tie him. Use the vines from the stretcher.
Hey, if we take that thing apart we won't be able to...
Ana, Sawyer needs a doctor or he'll die.
I hope this is all of it.
That's all we've got right now.
Hey, look who's here. Good to see you Ana Lucia, welcome back.
I'll get you those IRs, Captain.
Evidence? You're putting me behind a desk?
I don't care what you want.
Just put me in a car.
You were in an officer involve shooting -- no.
I need to be back on the street. Please.
I put you in a car -- everyone's going to know I pulled strings. You don't want me to treat you like my daughter? Don't ask for favors.
You don't get the transfer, but I'll give you the car. Happy?
Sorry to bother you Captain -- Ana, there's a call for you in the bullpen, they say it's real important.
Thanks, Raggs, I'll be right there.
They got you a cake, try and act surprised.
Jack, nice to see you out of the Hatch. You could use more sunshine.
A little fruit might do you good, too. Good for the constitution -- at least that's what they say.
Well, if that's what they say.
Well, now just because you're a little excited -- no reason to talk with your mouth full.
Past the hanging tree, double or nothing.
Dude, double or nothing, there's no way you're hitting it that far.
Hurley, the woman's on bloody steroids.
Oh, that's nothing to someone who's worth 150 million dollars. He'll build you your own course, if you'd like.
You hooked it. Try keeping your left arm straight.
So, you think you could do better?
Anyone can hit a ball. That's not golf.
Well, why don't we play a few holes and see which one of us is more accurate.
3 holes, no handicap, we play for bragging rights. What are we waiting for?
Ana, let's just get to their camp. It was an accident; they'll understand.
They'll understand? I killed one of them. If those two move, shout.
I'm taking him back to his camp.
Don't leave, please. If that were you, he'd let you die.
I'm not doing it for him. I'm doing it for me.
So'd the shrink give you any good drugs?
I heard about you and Danny. Want me to go kick his ass for you?
Nah. It's better this way. What are we doing in Westwood?
I thought a change of scenery would be nice -- a nice safe neighborhood for your first day back.
This was her idea, wasn't it?
809, please respond to a domestic disturbance call at 2210 South Veteran.
We're just 3 blocks from away from that call on Veteran. We got it.
Where are you going? What are you doing?! Drop the TV, Travis. The TV's mine.
I bought it with my money.
Do something useful, Shawna, shut your trap!
Arrest him; he is stealing my TV! He cannot get away with this!
She hit me! She hit me!
Hey, enough, all of you! Both of you!
Put your hands where I can see them! Get down on the ground!
Ana Lucia! He is down. Holster your weapon. Now.
Hey, I think you should let him go.
No. He's going to come after me.
I'm bringing him water. You going to shoot me? Shoot me.
Don't even think of untying him.
Her name is Ana Lucia. She was on the plane -- tail section -- they all were. We were bringing them back.
What do you mean they took him?
From the raft -- the night we left.
Hey, man. Hey, we're going to get out of this, man.
You know the lady's tee at my golf club back home is about 10 feet closer so if you...
Wow, you really put it in there.
We have more balls; you could always just take a drop.
You may want to move. What?
I got him. I got him.
Bathroom. He's burning up. We've got to get him in the shower -- bring his fever down.
Okay, go through the medical stash; there's a bottle of Ofloxacin...
Ofloxacin -- little white pills. Bring the alcohol and some gauge patches, and strip that bed and make sure it has clean sheets on it. Kate?!
Hey, what are we doing here, Ana? I mean, their camp has got to be close. I just want to get back to my wife.
How long have I kept you alive out here, Bernard? That's right. So cut me some slack.
Hey, we just want to know what you're doing. What's the plan?
She has no plan. She only has her guilt and a gun.
You want a plan? Do you have any ammo, back at your camp?
Ammo -- for the gun -- do you have any back at your camp?
Alright, I want it -- at least half. And I'll need a pack -- a big one -- a blanket, some medicine, clothes -- jeans and socks -- you getting all this?
Alright, you bring all of that stuff back to me and I'll let your friend go.
Go. 사이드를 절대로 다치게 할 수는 없어.
Ana, what are you doing? You can't live out here alone.
What, you talking to me, now?
I just want to go home A-L.
Hey, I ordered that guy to stop twice.
So does that mean every time we run into some yo-yo with a TV in his hands, you're going to draw down?
Hey, Ana Lucia -- your guy -- we got him.

His name is Jason McCormick. We picked him up on an assault on an elderly woman in Echo Park. His fingerprints matched a partial from your crime scene. We questioned him and he confessed. The D.A. is ready to file charges -- all he needs is for you to I.D. him.
Can you guys give us a minute? Why are you doing this?
He put 4 bullets into you -- hollow points through your vest -- he tried to kill you. You want a list of his priors? You want to put this guy back out on the street?
What happened? Out there -- what happened?
A girl was shot and killed.
She was tall with blonde hair. There was an Arab man...
Shannon. You said there were 5 of you from the tail section?
4 -- why didn't the rest of them come back with you? And Michael, Jin?
They cannot come back right now.
Can you take me to them?
Here you go. What's happening? Why is he shaking?
He's septic. The infection's gotten into the blood stream. If the anti-biotics don't bring the fever down he'll go into shock. Hold him up.
Swallow it, swallow it, swallow it.

I can do it. Sawyer? Hey, you have to listen to me, okay? The only way that you are going to get better is if you take this pill. So I want you to swallow it, okay? Okay, here we go. Now swallow, swallow. Good, good.
Nice job. I never learned the whole whisper-in-the-ear thing in med school.
I'm going, Ana. I don't want to be a part of this. I know everything you've done for us, and I wouldn't be alive if it weren't for you. But I'm going.
I just don't think you're the best judge of character. I was with you when you put Nathan in the pit.
Alright, you want to go? Go. Leave. You -- go.
Hi. Listen, it's alright. Jin is okay.
He's fine; and I'll explain. But right now, I need to find Jack.
Where are they? I said, where are they?
Shannon's dead! I'm not... You're going to take me back out there, right now.
This man isn't the problem. He brought Sawyer back.
Half-dead with a bullet hole in his shoulder, John! Are you going to talk to me or are you just going to sit there?
Anything I say will only make you angry. So, yes, I will sit here.
You remember how to get out there, right?
We should stop and think about this, Jack.
Think about what, John? Shannon's dead, Sayid's being held at gunpoint. You want to sit and hope that situation resolves itself -- be my guest. You know how to use this?
Stop! Please. What do you want?
Peace? Revenge? Justice? And you are going out with all these guns? What do you want?
I want all of our people back here safely. Your friend murdered-
I will take you there. But only you, and no guns.
Where are you from? Where are you from?
Why are you asking me if I have children?
I do not. Do you have children?
Are you going to kill me? That's what you're thinking about, isn't it?

Almost 40 days ago on this very Island I tied a man to a tree and tortured him. I tortured him as I've tortured many men -- men whose voices I still hear in the night. Should you kill me? Maybe you should. Maybe you were meant to.

I'm a cop... I was a cop. One night my partner and I responded to a burglary call. We were the first ones there. I covered the front and he went around the back. I was there a minute and then this kid comes right through the front door -- I tell him to put his hands up -- and he says I'm making a mistake -- that he's a student and he wants to show me his I.D. I believed him. I just -- I let him reach. All I remember was a pop. By the time I hit the ground I thought I was dead. I feel dead.
What happened to him -- the man who shot you?
Nothing. They never found him. Go ahead, pick it up, I deserve it.
What good it would be to kill you if we're both already dead?
Hey! Hey, can you hear me? You're going to be okay. You're going to be alright. You're home.
